terms.definition: Charity or donation. It means giving something to someone in need without expecting anything back. Daan can be in the form of money, food, clothes, knowledge, or even kindness.
terms.exampleUsage: गरीबों को दान देना एक अच्छा काम है। (Giving charity to the poor is a good deed.)

terms.definition: Account or calculation. It means keeping a record or count of things. In the story, it refers to keeping a tally of how much charity one has given.
terms.exampleUsage: उसने अपने खर्चे का हिसाब लगाया। (He calculated the account of his expenses.)

terms.definition: A generous person who gives charity. Someone who is known for being giving and helpful to others. A Daani shares what they have with those in need.
terms.exampleUsage: वह बहुत बड़ा दानी है, हमेशा सबकी मदद करता है। (He is a great giver, always helping everyone.)

terms.definition: Show-off or pretence. Doing something just to impress others rather than from genuine feeling. In the story, it refers to giving charity for the sake of being seen and praised.
terms.exampleUsage: दिखावे के लिए दान देना सच्चा दान नहीं है। (Giving charity for show is not true charity.)

terms.definition: Helping others selflessly. 'Par' means others and 'Upkaar' means good deed. So Paropkaar means doing good for others without any selfish motive.
terms.exampleUsage: परोपकार से बढ़कर कोई धर्म नहीं है। (There is no greater virtue than helping others selflessly.)

terms.definition: A person in need. Someone who requires help, whether it is food, money, clothes, or any other kind of support. Zaroorat means need, and -mand means having.
terms.exampleUsage: हमें ज़रूरतमंद लोगों की मदद करनी चाहिए। (We should help people in need.)

terms.definition: Generous or broad-minded. A person who gives freely and does not hesitate to share with others. An Udaar person has a big heart.
terms.exampleUsage: उदार व्यक्ति हमेशा दूसरों की मदद करता है। (A generous person always helps others.)

terms.definition: Feeling or emotion. The inner feeling or intention behind any action. In the context of charity, Bhaavna means the genuine feeling of wanting to help.
terms.exampleUsage: दान देने की भावना दिल से आनी चाहिए। (The feeling of giving charity should come from the heart.)

terms.definition: Miser or stingy person. Someone who does not like to spend or give away their things. The opposite of Daani (generous person).
terms.exampleUsage: कंजूस आदमी कभी दान नहीं देता। (A miserly person never gives charity.)

terms.definition: Service or selfless help. Serving others without expecting anything in return. Seva is a form of charity where you give your time and effort instead of money.
terms.exampleUsage: बड़ों की सेवा करना हमारा कर्तव्य है। (Serving our elders is our duty.)
